---
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

::: {style="text-align: center; color: #036ffc;"}
# IRE and Merrill College Data Journalism Training

### Dow Jones News Fund \@ Merrill College

#### Sunday, June 1 - Sunday, June 8, 2025

[(ver May 28, 2025)]{style="font-size: 8pt;"}
:::

::: {style="display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 20px; margin-top: 30px;"}
<img src="../images/DJNF_Logo.png" style="height: 170px; width: auto;"/>
<img src="../images/IRE_logo.png" style="height: 110px; width: auto;"/>
<img src="../images/Merrill_logo.png" style="height: 130px; width: auto;"/>
:::

::: {style="font-weight:bold;"}
``` markdown
Rob Wells, director
David Herzog, co-director
Karen Denny, co-director  
Bridget Lang, assistant  
```
:::

::: {style="text-align:center"}
<button><a href="https://djnf-data-2025.github.io/djnf_master_2025/Instructors">Instructors</a></button>

<button><a href="https://djnf-data-2025.github.io/djnf_master_2025/GuestSpeakers">Guest
Speakers</a></button>

<button><a href="https://djnf-data-2025.github.io/djnf_master_2025/Interns">Interns</a></button>
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; color: #036ffc;"}
# Itinerary
:::

**Pre-Conference**

-   **By Monday May 26**: Students were sent [data assignments and links
    to software to
    load](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NATRDMhUr8XSpkI-_bKfJCM8YVu9028QHKlk_7sbdw0/edit?usp=sharing).
-   **By May 29**: Students profiles due, 11:59 p.m.
-   Join [GroupMe for DJNF Data
    Journalism.](https://groupme.com/join_group/107365175/V7owSQCn)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Sunday, June 1, 2025**

-   **2 p.m.**: [Welcome at Knight Hall Eaton
    Theater.](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/13qVZOvplZblQLnlemfldcXSob73DVL-IlBBwuWBJxX8/edit?usp=sharing)
    -   Wells, Herzog, Denny, Lang, and Shirley Carswell, DJNF Executive
        Director
        -   [Wifi
            instructions](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1hsEDef008Ys1eX0ku4QuLP6mGLmuocsXoHoRRe2Gfmg/edit?usp=sharing)
            -   Logistics. The week ahead. Rooms, meals.
        -   Student introductions.
        -   [Investigate your
            community](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NATRDMhUr8XSpkI-_bKfJCM8YVu9028QHKlk_7sbdw0/edit?tab=t.0):
            Wells
            -   Link to [DJNF GitHub
                repositories](https://github.com/orgs/djnf-data-2025/repositories)

<!-- -->

-   **3:30 p.m.**: Use data to find stories.

    -   Spreadsheet track: Herzog. Emma Ferschweiler, Cooper Gant, Nate
        Harrington, Ava Hu, Ethan Hylton, Levi Jiang, Sophia Nabours.
        Eaton Theater\
    -   Coding track: Wells. Sasha Allen; Hannah Bensen; Olivia Borgula;
        Declan Bradley; Lauren Lifke; Apurva Mahajan; Patrick McCaslin;
        Diego Perdomo, Yasmeen Saadi. Room 2103
        -   [Handout for Coding Track
            talk](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wBIYilNkrB19WCQOeQKyLkLpw8z5Mx6O915BA4A9SpY/edit?usp=sharing)

-   **4:30 p.m.**: Break and then independent work - [Students review
    data
    memos](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1A-dlzWX7ksOt6LRHYpuPDk7SPPWxyZEmGt2wC0lM7Mg/edit)
    for their assigned communities. Produce draft story ideas by 11:59
    p.m.

-   **5:30 p.m.**: Break

-   **6:00 p.m.**: Dinner at Franklins in Hyattsville - (Meet at Knight
    Hall, board van)

-   **11:59 p.m.**: Produce one paragraph draft story idea by 11:59
    p.m., emailed to Wells & Herzog

> **Handouts, Readings for Sunday**

-   [Welcome
    Slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/13qVZOvplZblQLnlemfldcXSob73DVL-IlBBwuWBJxX8/edit?usp=sharing)

-   Link to [DJNF GitHub
    repositories](https://github.com/orgs/djnf-data-2025/repositories)

-   [Investigate your
    community](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NATRDMhUr8XSpkI-_bKfJCM8YVu9028QHKlk_7sbdw0/edit?tab=t.0)
    memo.

-   Link to [Jefferson, T. Letter to Edward Carrington, Paris, January
    16,
    1787](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1RowEaSMNvcNCNyH4XyftSSnMisDe8wg8/view?usp=sharing)

-   Link to [The Commission on the Freedom of the Press. Chapter 2: The
    requirements, pp.
    20-29.](https://drive.google.com/file/d/10FTgerZaMsgyxulhX9dNJPOUoWyA8ieR/view?usp=sharing)

-   [GitHub
    Slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1GofR1mONJ611ozikg08F-uuGalVoq2nua1j9sYfkJzo/edit?usp=sharing)

-   [Handout for Coding Track
    talk](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wBIYilNkrB19WCQOeQKyLkLpw8z5Mx6O915BA4A9SpY/edit?usp=sharing)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

**Monday, June 2, 2025**

-   **8:30 a.m. Breakfast**: At Knight Hall

-   **9 a.m.**: Meet at Knight Hall

-   **Skills sessions. Pick one**

    -   Coding Track: Data Driven Reporting. (Sean Mussenden, the Howard
        Center)
        -   Bring material into R and make sense of it.
        -   Basic webscraping, PDF data extraction
        -   Knight Hall Room 2103
    -   Spreadsheet Track: Pivot Tables. (Herzog, IRE)
        -   A powerful tool to summarize data\
        -   Knight Hall Room 2107

-   **10:30 a.m.**: Break. Coffee / drinks served. Lobby.

-   **11 a.m.**: Workshops: Use data to find stories. (Wells, Mussenden,
    Herzog). Rooms 2103, 2107

-   **12:30 p.m.**: Lunch in Knight Hall.

-   **1:30 p.m.**: Writing a story pitch memo. All attend. (Denny).
    Eaton Theater

    -   [Slides for
        talk](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1zdlk2Ss9jE_SZL2HuHvUbZeUteEO0OBasFKpXtjuZIY/edit?usp=sharing)

-   **2:15 p.m.**: Hosting a webpage on GitHub. All attend.
    (Wells/Lang). Eaton Theater

    -   [Web page hosting
        tutorial](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1m6WrprX3BshPV3r0YVrx5fol8reB2azS8Mb19lN6qkg/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.ahlwvelrtcgr)

    -   [GitHub
        refresher](https://wellsdata.github.io/data_journalism_class/_book/github.html).

-   **2:45 p.m.**: Workshop for [story pitch memo due at 11:59
    p.m.](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1A-dlzWX7ksOt6LRHYpuPDk7SPPWxyZEmGt2wC0lM7Mg/edit)
    (Wells, Denny, Herzog, TA coach). Rooms 2103, 2107

-   **4 p.m.**: Diversity is Good Journalism: Covering All of the
    Community. (Prof. Christoph Mergerson). All attend. Room 2103

    -   Link to [Jefferson, T. Letter to Edward Carrington, Paris,
        January 16,
        1787](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1RowEaSMNvcNCNyH4XyftSSnMisDe8wg8/view?usp=sharing)

    -   Link to [The Commission on the Freedom of the Press. Chapter 2:
        The requirements, pp.
        20-29](https://drive.google.com/file/d/10FTgerZaMsgyxulhX9dNJPOUoWyA8ieR/view?usp=sharing)

-   **5:30 p.m.**: Break

-   **6:00 p.m.**: Meet at Knight Hall and walk to [Cava, College
    Park.](https://cava.com/locations/college-park-md)

-   **11:59 p.m.**: Draft story pitch memo emailed to Wells and Herzog.

    Monday, June 3, 11:59 p.m. Draft a story pitch memo.

–Submit as a link to an R Markdown document on your GitHub account.

A minimum of 400 words, building on the story idea paragraph, that
incorporates some of the best practices in pitching a story and
generally follows the story pitch template and process as described in
Monday’s lecture. This draft pitch will reflect a deeper exploration of
the assigned data and hopefully some independent research. The best
story pitches will have some context about recent coverage of this
topic.

> **Handouts, Readings for Monday** -

-   [Readings for Cheryl W. Thompson
    talk](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1P6RsrtnL0TJY0-jOc3QzAR8XFifecfQQSO5GTp0kRfA/edit?usp=sharing)

-   [Slides for Karen Denny
    talk](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1zdlk2Ss9jE_SZL2HuHvUbZeUteEO0OBasFKpXtjuZIY/edit?usp=sharing)

-   [Web page hosting
    tutorial](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1m6WrprX3BshPV3r0YVrx5fol8reB2azS8Mb19lN6qkg/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.ahlwvelrtcgr)

-   [Sean Mussenden R Intro](https://go.umd.edu/djnf_r_intro)

-   [Christoph Mergerson
    slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1842t-NVaVTc4aGFKeZv7PAxVYw01JNGy/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=102324743793755798467&rtpof=true&sd=true)

-   Christoph Mergerson contact:
    [cm495\@umd.edu](mailto:cm495@umd.edu){.email}

-   Cheryl W. Thompson contact:
    [cwthompson\@npr.org](mailto:cwthompson@npr.org){.email}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

**Tuesday June 3, 2025**

-   **8:00 a.m. Breakfast**: At Knight Hall

-   **Heather Taylor, DJ News Fund visiting**

-   **9 a.m.-10:15 a.m.: Data Visualization.** Using Datawrapper for
    data visualization. (Adam Marton). All attend. Eaton Theater

-   **10:30 a.m.**: Break. Coffee served.

    -   Heather Taylor, manager of digital media and programs, Dow Jones
        News Fund.

-   **11 a.m.**: Workshop. Students work on data gathering story
    pitches. (Wells, Marton, TA as coaches). Eaton Theater

-   **11:45 p.m. - 12:45 p.m.**: Guest speaker: [Cheryl W. Thompson,
    investigative correspondent for
    NPR](https://www.npr.org/people/705265133/cheryl-w-thompson). All
    attend. Eaton Theater.

    -   [Readings before Cheryl Thompson
        talk](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1P6RsrtnL0TJY0-jOc3QzAR8XFifecfQQSO5GTp0kRfA/edit?usp=sharing)

-   **12:45 p.m. - 1:30 p.m.** Lunch with Cheryl Thompson. Sandwiches
    served in Knight Hall.

-   **1:30 p.m. - 3:00 p.m.: Skills sessions**

    -   Coding Track: Using AI with R. (Derek Willis, the Howard Center)
        -   Using AI to extract information from PDFs/images/video/audio
        -   Knight Hall Room 2103
    -   Spreadsheet Track: Data cleaning. (Herzog, IRE)
        -   Use Google Sheets, Open Refine to clean data
        -   Knight Hall Room 2107

-   **3:00 p.m.-3:15 p.m.** : Break

-   **3:15 p.m.-4:15 p.m.**

    -   Mapping. Herzog.
        -   Make a map in Datawrapper.\
        -   Knight Hall Room 2103
    -   Workshop on AI (Willis / Wells /Lang). Room 2107

-   **4:15 p.m.-5:30 p.m.** Workshop

    -   [Git
        Ignore!](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bs1CQXogrW4Qy-0rNh87vtAgoQe1qy3JHToQqwqmHAI/edit?usp=sharing)

-   **5:30 p.m.**: Break

-   **6:30 p.m.**: Dinner at [Greene Turtle, College Park, 7356
    Baltimore Ave, College Park, MD
    20740.](https://www.thegreeneturtle.com/menu/) (Meet at Knight Hall,
    walk to venue)

> **Handouts, Readings for Tuesday** -

-   [Slides from Cheryl W. Thompson
    talk](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1nO41TYeSyoC-p0Xdn7pH872OVkZxdr3M/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=102324743793755798467&rtpof=true&sd=true)
-   Cheryl W Thompson email:
    [cwthompson\@npr.org](mailto:cwthompson@npr.org){.email}
-   Derek Willis [AI and coding
    lecture](https://github.com/profrobwells/djnf-llm-willis-2025)
-   Adam Marton's visualization
    [slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1RKI9xYdT1qPcg2iknl3H4R-x7pxNeJcdycjmNxO9no8/edit?usp=sharing)
    and [Datawrapper API
    repo](https://amarton.github.io/datawrapper-api/)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

**Wednesday June 4, 2025**

-   **8:30 a.m. Breakfast**: At Knight Hall

-   **9 a.m.-10:15 a.m.: Skills sessions**

    -   Coding Track: Using AI with R. (Daniel Trielli, Merrill College)
        -   Use R to link to a LLM and clean data.
        -   Knight Hall Room 2103
    -   Spreadsheet Track: File Types and Metadata. (Herzog, IRE)
        -   Use Microsoft Visual Studio to identify file types
        -   Knight Hall Room 2107

-   **10:15 a.m.**: Break. Coffee served.

-   **10:30 a.m.**: Workshop. Open workshop on projects. (Wells, Herzog,
    Lang coach). Rooms 2103, 2107

-   **12 p.m.**: Lunch in Knight Hall.

    -   Brief remarks by Alex Pyles, Associate Dean, Academic Affairs,
        Philip Merrill College of Journalism

-   **1 p.m. - 2:15 p.m.**: Covering Congress remotely, Ed Kachinske,
    U.S. House Press Gallery, via Zoom. Eaton Theater

    -   Exercise: [Examine spending by a member of
        Congress](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1QUMXabTndXJipJl-erktV2l6OZSHWVgpIq0AHv9p-44/edit?usp=sharing)

-   **2:15 p.m. - 2:30 p.m.**: Break

-   **2:30 p.m. - 3:30 p.m.** Connie Mitchell Ford on business
    reporting. All attend. Eaton Theater.

    -   Three ways to get business and economic data and how to localize
        it.

-   **3:30 p.m.**: Workshop. Connie Mitchell Ford workshop on project
    proposals. (Wells, Lang, Herzog as coaches). Eaton Theater

-   **5 p.m.**: Break

-   **6:00 p.m.**: Dinner delivered to Knight Hall. [Taqueria
    Habanero](https://www.taqueriahabanero.com/)

-   **11:59 p.m.**: [Second draft of story pitch memos
    due](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1A-dlzWX7ksOt6LRHYpuPDk7SPPWxyZEmGt2wC0lM7Mg/edit),
    emailed to Wells and Herzog.

> **Handouts, Readings for Wednesday** -
>
> -   [Daniel Trielli
>     slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1GaN2LlIBD05ReyeHSAl4bhnBAwdf47W5byoHEwLUEaA/edit?usp=sharing)
>
> -   [Constance Ford
>     slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/19Zbbdl59VLVdcgjBWIwvk7HbaC2r-RAy/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=102324743793755798467&rtpof=true&sd=true)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

**Thursday June 5, 2025**

-   **7:45 a.m. Breakfast**: At Knight Hall
-   **8:15 a.m.**: Meet at Knight Hall, board van, depart for College
    Park Metro (Wells/Herzog driving)
-   **10 a.m.**: Tour of AP D.C. bureau with Chad Day, AP's elections
    team.
-   **Noon**: Lunch.
-   **1:30 p.m.**: Tour U.S. Capitol press galleries with Stephen
    Neukam, Axios and Ed Kachinske, U.S. House Press Gallery.
-   **6 p.m.**: Dinner in Washington D.C.

> **Handouts, Readings for Thursday** -
>
> -   Chad Day, AP: cday\@ap.org
>
> -   Gary Fields, AP: GFields\@AP.org
>
> -   Stephen Neukam, Axios: stephen.neukam\@axios.com
>
> -   Ed Kachinske, House Press Gallery:
>     Edward.Kachinske\@mail.house.gov

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

**Friday June 6, 2025**

-   **8:30 a.m. Breakfast**: At Knight Hall
-   **9 a.m.: Gathering Public Records**
    -   All attend. (Herzog and Wells). Eaton Theater
-   **10:00 a.m. - 10:15 a.m.**: Break
-   **10:15 a.m.**: Workshop on projects.
-   **11:15 a.m.**: Skills to survive in the newsroom. Time management,
    deadlines. All attend. (Denny) Eaton Theater
-   **12 p.m.**: Lunch in Knight Hall.
    -   Speaker: Prof. Kevin Blackistone on racial justice in sports
        business
-   **1 p.m.**: Workshop on projects (Wells, Herzog, Denny, Lang coach)
-   **2:45 p.m.**: Meet at Knight Hall, board van, depart for College
    Park Metro, dinner in Washington D.C., attend a community jazz show
    at [Westminster Church](https://westminsterdc.org/our-pastor).
-   **4:30 p.m.** Meet with Rev. Brian Hamilton at [Westminster
    Church](https://westminsterdc.org/our-pastor) to discuss Southwest
    D.C. history.
-   **6 p.m.** Jazz show and dinner at Westminister Church: [The
    Organization](https://mailchi.mp/westminsterdc.org/jazz-night-in-dc-july-16-2021-resurrection-jazz-night-returns-16366988?e=11ceb88ef9).
    Greg Hatza, organ; Brian Kooken, guitar; Robert Shahid, drums; Dred
    Scott, vocals

> **Handouts, Readings for Friday** -
>
> -   David Herzog, [Open
>     Records](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1V9A96-4y-EH_0Ce_XzQl2gfwfzvVTHwU/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=102324743793755798467&rtpof=true&sd=true)
>
> -   Karen Denny, [How to Succeed in a Newsroom]
>     <https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1D-E8yd_BARkJ100iMOjedThaHBvbD15qPrv0IclgmV8/edit?usp=sharing>
>
> -   Kevin Blackistone, [Sports and Environmental
>     Justice](https://cnsmaryland.org/2024/12/11/big-ten-football-travel-carbon-emissions-expansion/)
>
> -   Kevin Blackistone
>     [columns](https://www.washingtonpost.com/people/kevin-b-blackistone/)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

**Saturday June 7, 2025**

-   **9:00 a.m. Breakfast**: At Knight Hall

-   **10:00 a.m.**: [Students present web
    pages](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1A-dlzWX7ksOt6LRHYpuPDk7SPPWxyZEmGt2wC0lM7Mg/edit),
    story pitches, solicit feedback.

-   **12:15 p.m.**: Lunch in Knight Hall

-   **1 p.m.**: On the van for D.C.

-   **2:15 p.m.**: Tour of African-American History Museum, D.C.

-   **6:00 p.m.**: Dinner at Ledo Pizza, College Park

> **Handouts, Readings for Saturday** - TK

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

**Sunday June 8, 2025**

-   Breakfast on your own.
-   Depart on your own schedule

# Photos

DJNF photos by [Kate
DeBlasis](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1hKpFRi3-EvZtahdhMuRinPkn6KquBT-j?usp=drive_link),
Philip Merrill College of Journalism

Wells [photos from DC and
beyond](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1TAzfv3yEHd2tfYrfPw9lxgHBMJOu9jfs?usp=sharing)

# Resources

::: <br> **Data Journalism Text**

[Data Journalism with R and the
Tidyverse](https://wellsdata.github.io/data_journalism_class/_book/)

<br>

### Important Contacts

<br>

**Rob Wells, (443) 591-1189. email:
[robwells\@umd.edu](mailto:robwells@umd.edu){.email}**

<br>

**David Herzog, (573) 356-3029. email:
[herzogd\@missouri.edu](mailto:herzogd@missouri.edu){.email}**

<br>

**Karen Denny, (301) 775-8483. email:
[kdenny12\@umd.edu](mailto:kdenny12@umd.edu){.email}**

**Residence Halls & Campus**

<div>

-   You are staying in Dorchester Hall, a very short walk to Knight
    Hall. The 24-hospitality (check-in/out) desk is located in Queen
    Anne’s Hall, a short walk to the south. The desk phone number is
    301-314-4455 (HILL) for key- and access-related questions. The Stamp
    Student Union, with a food court and various retail, is near
    Dorchester Hall.

**Campus Map**

<img src="../images/campus_map_2025.PNG"/>

<br>

**Residence Hall Contacts**

-   Jennifer Bradley Senior Program Manager Office:301-314-0323 Mobile:
    301-440-9276 Email:
    [jarsen17\@umd.edu](mailto:jarsen17@umd.edu){.email}

-   Emely Victoria Munoz-Ramos, Assistant Program Manager: 301-314-3888.
    Email: [emramos\@umd.edu](mailto:emramos@umd.edu){.email}

-   Jed Silverman, Assistant Program Manager. Email:
    [jed\@umd.edu](mailto:jed@umd.edu){.email}

-   Residential Facilities (To report an issue with your room/residence
    hall) 301-314-9675 (WORK) Campus Information 301-405-1000

</div>

**Medical/Emergency Contacts**

<div>

-   Emergency (University of Maryland Police) 301-405-3333
-   Emergency (Prince George’s County Police) 911
-   Non-Emergency (University of Maryland Police) 301-405-3555
-   University Health Center 301-314-8180

</div>
